partial_progress.details: Phase 6b blocked: Attempted direct proof for M-reflexive cases. Proved V reflexive from M reflexive via T4 chain. However, cannot derive 'phi in M implies phi in V' without phi->G(phi) axiom (not valid in temporal logic). Recursive calls to density_frame_condition_strict fail termination checker.

completion_data.completion_summary: Iteration 3 analysis: Proved V reflexive when M reflexive and V ~ M using T4 chain. Core blocking issue: proving phi in V from phi in M requires phi->G(phi) which is not a temporal axiom. The iteration pattern needs proper well-founded recursion with subformulaClosure termination measure.
completion_data.key_insight: The reflexive equivalence class M ~ V shares reflexivity (proved via T4), but membership phi in M does not imply phi in V without G(phi) in M. The iteration must escape via seriality to find a witness outside the equivalence class.

review_reason: All 16 sorries require implementing well-founded iteration with Nat.strongRecOn on subformulaClosure cardinality. Direct proof approaches fail because temporal logic lacks phi -> G(phi) axiom. Options: (1) Implement fuel_suffices theorem with proper termination proof, (2) Axiomatize strict density property, (3) Alternative mathematical approach using MCS construction variants.
